questions.q: What dietary recommendation did the American Heart Association recently make regarding protein and dairy?
options: Favor plant-based proteins and replace full-fat dairy with low-fat or non-fat dairy.
Increase consumption of red meat and full-fat dairy for better heart health.
Focus on lean meats and eliminate all dairy products.
Eat more processed foods and sugary drinks, as they have no impact on heart health.
questions.correctText: The American Heart Association urged people to favor plant-based proteins over meat and replace full-fat dairy with non-fat and low-fat dairy options.

questions.q: Approximately how many years has humanity been actively fighting against HIV/AIDS, according to recent reports?
questions.correctText: Recent reports indicate that humanity has been actively fighting against HIV/AIDS for approximately 45 years, with HIV first identified in 1981.

questions.q: Which pharmaceutical company recently received FDA approval for the new GLP-1 weight loss pill named Foundayo?
questions.correctText: Eli Lilly recently received FDA approval for Foundayo (orforglipron), a new GLP-1 weight loss pill.

questions.q: What did a doctors' group recently clarify about the American Medical Association's (AMA) stance on gender-affirming care for minors?
options: The AMA has completely reversed its support for gender-affirming care for minors.
The AMA's guidance on gender-affirming care for minors remains unchanged.
The AMA now only supports surgical interventions for minors.
The AMA has deferred all decisions on gender-affirming care for minors to state governments.
questions.correctText: A doctors' group clarified that the American Medical Association's (AMA) guidance on gender-affirming care for minors remains unchanged, despite some media reports suggesting a retreat.

questions.q: What type of common chemical, often found in plastics, has recently been linked to millions of preterm births and thousands of infant deaths?
questions.correctText: A new study has linked phthalates, a type of chemical commonly used to make plastic more flexible, to millions of preterm births and thousands of infant deaths.
